target.name: Lightelligence
target.founder: Shen Yichen, Marin Soljačić, Huaiyu Meng, Spencer Powers

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lightelligence is a public-stage semiconductor company that has raised $270M+ to date. The peer universe for valuation consists of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Cerebras ($23.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B). This set spans a wide range, from $1.0B to $23.0B, reflecting meaningful dispersion in how public semiconductor startups are currently valued.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach, yielding an implied valuation of $3.2B based on the median across the four comparables. A revenue-based public-comp anchoring analysis using semiconductor peers (NVDA, AMD, ALAB at a median 20.6x P/S) cannot be applied, as target revenue is not disclosed. This is typical for private companies, though Lightelligence's current public status may eventually enable this method.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $3.7B, centered on the $3.2B peer median. Key uncertainties include the wide spread within the peer set itself—a four-fold variance between the highest and lowest comps—and the absence of revenue-based metrics, which limits confidence in fundamental anchoring. The $3.2B median reflects market pricing for public-stage semiconductor innovators but does not account for company-specific traction, technology defensibility, or capital efficiency relative to peers.
